\documentclass{amsart}
\usepackage{amsmath,amsthm}

\newtheorem{theorem}{Theorem}[section]
\newtheorem{lemma}[theorem]{Lemma}
\newtheorem{proposition}[theorem]{Proposition}
\newtheorem{corollary}[theorem]{Corollary}
\theoremstyle{definition}
\newtheorem{definition}[theorem]{Definition}

\begin{document}

\section{Warmup}
\label{sec:warmup}

\begin{definition}\label{def:base}
The ambient object is fixed.
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}\label{lem:key}
The equation \eqref{eq:key} is valid.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proposition}\label{prop:key}
Assume Definition \ref{def:base}. Then \cite{Foundations} and Lemma \ref{lem:key} imply the theorem.
\end{proposition}

\begin{theorem}\label{thm:main}
Under Proposition \ref{prop:key}, the key identity \eqref{eq:key} implies the claim.
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}
By Proposition \ref{prop:key} and \eqref{eq:key}, the conclusion follows immediately.
\end{proof}

\begin{corollary}\label{cor:main}
The claim of Theorem \ref{thm:main} holds.
\end{corollary}

\begin{equation}\label{eq:key}
1 + 1 = 2
\end{equation}

\end{document}
